title: Blankets needed to save hundreds of lives
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Steri Squad, a volunteer...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Steri Squad, a volunteer group dedicated to animal welfare, specifically focusing on sterilisation campaigns, is calling on the community to donate old blankets and towels for the upcoming SPCA Spay-a-thon in Golden Valley, Blanco, between 10 and 14 March.


> These items will provide much-needed comfort to animals undergoing sterilisation.


They are also looking for animal-loving sponsors to help them reach their goal of sterilising 150 animals in March.

Every contribution counts - even R20 can make a difference.

R450 per animal covers sterilisation, vaccination and deworming, helping reduce stray populations and improve animal welfare.

How you can help

Drop off blankets and towels at the SPCA Charity Shop at the York Mall arcade, 100 York Street, or phone 071 658 7353.


> Drop-offs can also be made at the Garden Route SPCA in Ossie Urban Road.


‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
